items.question: Why use a virtual environment at all?
items.answer: To isolate each project's dependencies so different projects can use different (even conflicting) package versions without affecting one another or the system Python.

items.question: venv vs virtualenv — which should you reach for?
items.answer: Use venv by default since it ships with Python. Choose virtualenv when you need its faster creation, extra features, or support for older Python versions.

items.question: What breaks if you pip install without activating the env?
items.answer: The package installs into system (or user) Python, so the project's interpreter still can't import it — and you may pollute or break the system environment.
